name: Tadalafil improved urinary symptom quality-of-life measures in BPH/LUTS men without ED
statement: In a pooled analysis of BPH/LUTS randomized trials, tadalafil 5 mg once daily improved IPSS quality-of-life and BPH Impact Index scores versus placebo in men without ED, but these are urinary symptom-specific quality-of-life outcomes in older BPH patients.
population_or_context: Men at least 45 years old with BPH/LUTS; subgroup without ED.
quantitative_note: In men without ED, IPSS-QoL improved -1.0 with tadalafil versus -0.7 with placebo and BII improved -1.4 versus -1.0; both P<0.05.

source_locator.quote_or_paraphrase_note: Abstract reports IPSS, IPSS-QoL, and BII subgroup results for men without ED.

population_profile: Men with BPH/LUTS, age at least 45, including subgroup without ED.
tadalafil_exposure: Tadalafil 5 mg once daily for 12 weeks.
endpoint_measure: IPSS quality-of-life subscore and BPH Impact Index.
effect_estimate: IPSS-QoL -1.0 vs -0.7 and BII -1.4 vs -1.0 for tadalafil versus placebo in men without ED; both P<0.05.
safety_findings: Abstract reports tadalafil was safe and well tolerated, with adverse-event profile consistent with men with ED.
directness_to_target_population: Low because participants had BPH/LUTS and were older than the primary target population.
confounding_and_bias_limits: Quality of life likely reflects urinary symptom relief rather than broad well-being or mood.
class_transfer_limits: Direct tadalafil evidence; no class transfer, but endpoint transfer from urinary symptom burden to broad well-being is limited.
